hook_text: They said our pricing was too high. Six months later, they called back. Same pricing.
body:
March 2025. Discovery call with an Oman manufacturing company. 160 employees.

Good conversation. They understood the product. They liked what they saw.

Then: "We'll stick with our current system for now. Your price is too high."

Current system: a mix of Excel, a legacy Gulf HR product, and one dedicated payroll staff member.

I thanked them and moved on.

September 2025. They called back.

What changed?

- Two WPS rejections in one quarter
- One MOL fine for incorrect Omanization reporting
- Their payroll staff member resigned (took institutional knowledge with her)
- Six weeks trying to train a replacement on the manual process

Total cost in 6 months: OMR 1,400 in fines + estimated OMR 800 in lost productivity.

Our annual contract: OMR 1,200.

"You're actually the cheaper option," their finance director said.

They went live in October.

---

Software always looks expensive until you price the alternative.

What's the real cost of your current HR system?
rejected_reason: pre-PR#12 backlog purge — queue hygiene 2026-07-15 (regenerate via corrected pipeline)
